purpose: VS Code-style debug sidebar with four collapsible sections: Variables, Call Stack, Breakpoints, Watch Expressions. Reads exclusively from AppContext debug state (debugSession, debugScopes, debugVariables, debugCallStack, debugBreakpoints, debugWatchExpressions). VariablesSection iterates debugScopes and renders VariableRow recursively; rows with variable.variablesReference > 0 are lazy-expanded by calling window.electronAPI.debugGetVariables(variablesReference) on first click and caching children locally. CallStackSection lists frames; clicking a frame calls onNavigateToFrame(source, line, column) prop (wired by IDEView to dispatch IDE_OPEN_FILE + ideNavigateToLine). BreakpointsSection flattens debugBreakpoints (Object<filePath, line[]>) into a sorted list; checkbox click dispatches DEBUG_TOGGLE_BREAKPOINT, row click navigates. WatchSection adds expressions that are evaluated immediately via window.electronAPI.debugEvaluate (using top-of-stack callFrameId when paused) and stored in debugWatchExpressions; results are NOT auto-refreshed on pause changes — they capture the value at insertion time.
gotchas: Variables only render when debugSession.status === 'paused' — when paused, debugScopes drives the layout, but if debugVariables[scope.variablesReference] hasn't been populated by IDEView's debug-paused handler, the row shows 'No variables' even though scopes exist.

Lazy variable expansion does NOT re-fetch on subsequent expand — once children loaded, they're cached in component state for the lifetime of the row. Resuming and re-pausing creates new variablesReference IDs (DAP/V8 inspector spec), so old cached values are simply unmounted on the next debug-paused dispatch when scopes change.
Watch expressions are static snapshots — entered values do NOT auto-refresh when the debugger steps. Users must remove and re-add to refresh.

BreakpointsSection's DEBUG_TOGGLE_BREAKPOINT dispatch uses a different shape than CodeEditor's: { filePath, line } vs { filePath, lineNumber, enabled }. Reducer must accept both or breakpoints will desync.
Watch expression IDs use Date.now()+random — fast double-clicks could collide (rare).
Truncation at 80 chars (60 for watch values) is hardcoded — long values are silently elided with '\u2026' and shown in title attribute on hover.
patterns: CollapsibleSection is a reusable inner component — used by all four sections for consistent header/chevron/count UI.
VariableRow is recursive: renders itself for each child after lazy-loaded expansion; depth controls paddingLeft visually (12 + depth * 16).
Empty/disabled state uses 'dbg-empty' class with messages like 'Not paused', 'No call frames', 'No breakpoints set'.
Navigation callbacks (onNavigateToFrame, onNavigateToBreakpoint) are PROP-DRIVEN — the panel itself never dispatches navigation; IDEView wires up the file-open + ideNavigateToLine flow.
conventions: Inner SVG icon components (ChevronIcon, CheckboxCheckedIcon, AddIcon, RemoveIcon) follow currentColor stroke pattern.
Section CSS uses 'dbg-' prefix (dbg-section, dbg-var-row, dbg-stack-row, dbg-bp-row, dbg-watch-row).
Each section's JSX is wrapped in CollapsibleSection title + count props.
